sentences.text: Mike, I think you've met Justin Davidson in the past.
Yeah.
Great to see you again, Mike.
How's it going?
Going good, sir.
And then I also, our VP of product who I've mentioned in the past, Megan, she'll join probably about halfway through the call.
Um, she had a little bit of overlay, but definitely wanted to, to meet you all, so we're excited.
Thank you.
And from our team we have, uh,

myself, um, Bela Kaongo, he's our director for multi-family strategy and analytics, and we have our investment leadership team here with, uh, Mike Campbell, um, and Everett as well joining us because they use some other platforms when they're doing acquisitions and due diligence.
So I thought that this might be a good opportunity for them to understand apartment, um, IQ's platform and how that can give them the value proposition.
I'm waiting for our asset management team, Devin, so he just joined and also he's our vice president of asset management and, uh, they're in critical properties.
They manage the inventory pricing through our team, but it will give them probably much more visibility through apartments, um, through your apartment IQ platform.
So that's all I have to say here.
We appreciate that.
It's uh nice to meet
you both, Mike and Devin.
Likewise, same here.
Are you both in the Boston
area, where are you guys calling from?
Yeah, we're, we're all based out
of the Newton office.
OK, awesome.
Well, I got the pleasure of meeting David
a few weeks ago too.
So um it's been nice to get to meet everybody throughout the spent a couple of years it feels like so nice to put a face to name with you guys.
Perfect.
Nice to meet you as well.

Yeah, I, I guess as far as kind of um as Mak was kind of mentioning in the, in the intro, um happy to show you, you know, all the capabilities of of apartment IQ, but I guess, you know, just to maximize your guys' time and obviously did my research on, you know, your guys's background, um, did anything in particular kind of pique your interest or I guess, um, Any like burning questions that you have off the bat.
I guess I will let Mike
or just chime in when they feel comfortable about it.
Uh, for us it was at least from our team, my team, revenue and strategy.
It's primarily the price points which the competitor is renting at, what is their specials, how fast they're leasing, so all that information is.
Um, you're getting to us on a daily basis through weekly reports.
Um, I think from their perspective it is about the market information, um, and Devin and Mike, feel free to chime in here.

I'm thinking that they're more interested in the market level information and also about properties which we may not have in our portfolio and it can get some more granular information on potential acquisition targets or potential information about.
What's going on in that specific market where we may or may not have a footprint.
So that's probably one area of opportunity for us.
Yeah, I think from our perspective,
we're always looking at potential resources for data to understand the concept against everything that we're underwriting or how we're performing, um, and to help influence our assumptions.
So to the extent you guys get into anything on That front, that'd be helpful.
I've not seen the platform at all at any point, so I, I'm not really sure what I'm in for, to be honest.
Sure.
Well, uh, anything on your end, Devin
before we happy to, you know, give you the rundown on everything when we definitely kind of check those boxes that you mentioned, Mike, um, Devin, anything there, so you're coming off mute.
No, I, I, I was just gonna
say I, I echo what Mike says.
I was about to say that I haven't seen the platform, so, um, coming in a little bit, um, you know, without any preconceived notions, but definitely market data.
You know, in particular, you know, what are, what are advertised market rents, specials, um,
Any way to infer leasing velocity of our competitors.
All that type of information is interesting to me.
Great, we definitely can hit hit hit on that.
So, um, I guess a little background if you haven't heard on us apartment IQ.
So we started an over 12 year old company here, um, really kind of the creator of the daily automated market survey tool in the multi-family space.
Um, and what really makes us unique is that all of our information is updated daily.
So you're going to get much more live, uh, data compared to, you know, Maukreli has filled me in.
On his processes and his team as far as kind of the manual process or a few of the other providers that you've used in the past.
Uh, so the big difference is all of our information is updated daily, uh, and it's 100% public sources as well.
So I know it's important for a lot of folks, um.
In particular, so, uh, and what, what that's led to be is, uh, you know, now we work with over 35 of the top 50 and 7 of the top 10.
So, um, it's really ingrained and enriched in these organizations from the site level all the way up to executives like yourself.
So as you'll see.

Um, we'll have a portion that is just really easy to use, intuitive that, you know, your site teams, your on-site managers can use, um, and then we have kind of that, uh, you know, development and acquisitions portion of the platform as well that you can identify new deals, you can see kind of more of a market trend, you can see uh how time, you know, basically leasing has been over, over 4 years.
So that's really kind of um The key advantage to apartment IQ is we have all this public data over 4 years available at your fingertips.
So, um, really a full suite as far as being available from, uh, you know, kind of the existing portfolio all the way over to acquisitions and development.
Um, so that's kind of like I guess kind of the elevator pitch.
Um, I'll get into just sharing what that looks like, but I guess I'm curious, Mike and Devin, do you guys
Leverage one tool over the other?
Is it kind of a a mixture, I guess, what do you guys usually lean on?
Uh, for external data, we lean on
Co-star.

OK, that makes sense.

And then specifically like with your existing portfolio like your market surveys, we've also heard like concessions coverage as you as you've mentioned is kind of difficult there.
